programme.title: Hazardous History With Henry Winkler
programme.desc: The modern concept of extreme sports is a product of marketing, but back in the old days, the lack of rules made them truly extreme; from bone-crushing football and deadly baseball games to wild events like auto polo.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: History is full of miracle cures, far-fetched products, and astonishing feats, but not all of them are legitimate; a miracle snake oil that cures all? A piece of the Brooklyn Bridge for sale? A marathon-winner who crosses the finish line without hardly breaking a sweat? These are the stories of some of history's most spectacular cons, each deception more unbelievable than the last.

programme.title: Secrets Declassified With David Duchovny
programme.desc: Step inside hidden areas where history was steered in secret, from Churchill's wartime bunker, complete with a direct line to FDR concealed inside his private bathroom, to a Soviet weapons base in the desert exposed only by a daring U-2 spy mission.

programme.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: A shot rings out from the balcony of the Ford's Theater in Washington, D.C., and America's 16th president—Abraham Lincoln—has been killed. It has been long thought that this was the work of one man... but some believe the assassination was part of a larger plot months in the making. Who was behind the plan to assassinate President Lincoln?

programme.title: History's Deadliest With Ving Rhames
programme.desc: From doomed voyages across continents and oceans to covert CIA operations, these missions show that no matter how much preparation and planning are done, things can still turn deadly.
